items.title: Gucci's Second Monte Carlo Campaign Chapter Drops with Giglio, Jackie, Dionysus
items.summary: US trade roundup covers Gucci's second Monte Carlo campaign chapter starring Amelia Gray and Anok Yai, transitioning from coastal daywear to late-summer evening looks. New metallic-mesh Jackie and shimmering Dionysus bags are featured alongside the Giglio, tied to the 60th anniversary of the Flora motif.

items.title: Gucci's Monte Carlo Second Chapter Moves Resort Dressing Indoors Under Demna
items.summary: NY-based style outlet analyzes how Demna's second Monte Carlo campaign chapter shifts the mood from beach glamour to evening interiors, reading it as a signal of how the new creative direction is translating resort dressing for the US market ahead of fall retail.

items.title: WWD Digital Daily, July 13, 2026 - Gucci Monte Carlo Accessories Update
items.summary: WWD's Monday trade digest notes Gucci's latest Monte Carlo accessories drop, including the metallic-mesh Jackie and shimmering Dionysus, as part of its roundup of fashion-industry moves for the week of July 13.

items.title: Aryna Sabalenka, Jannik Sinner Disrupt Tennis Marketing via Gucci
items.summary: Sports-business feature on how Gucci's endorsement deals with Sabalenka and Sinner are reshaping tennis apparel marketing; cited again this week as Sabalenka's Gucci ties draw fresh attention during her Wimbledon run against Naomi Osaka.

items.title: Wimbledon 2026 Best-Dressed: Sabalenka's Gucci Ties Featured Among Top Looks
items.summary: Forbes' Wimbledon style roundup includes Aryna Sabalenka among memorable player looks, referencing her Gucci brand relationship as part of the broader tennis-fashion crossover narrative running through the tournament this month. A Wimbledon crowd also booed Sabalenka this week after her round-of-16 loss to Naomi Osaka, an incident circulating widely in US sports/entertainment feeds alongside her Gucci association.

items.title: Law Firm Investigating Kering Over Alleged Tariff Price-Hike 'Double Dip' at Gucci
items.summary: US law firm Edelson Lechtzin's ongoing investigation into whether Kering raised Gucci, Saint Laurent, and Balenciaga prices to cover Trump-era tariffs, then failed to refund customers after the Supreme Court struck the tariffs down in February 2026. No suit filed yet; firm is soliciting affected US consumers, keeping the story circulating on consumer-law sites this week.

items.title: PurseForum Thread: 2026 Gucci Flora Pieces Under Demna
items.summary: Active PurseForum thread where US handbag collectors discuss the new Flora-print totes, bucket bags, and pouches released under Demna, comparing coated-canvas and nylon versions to earlier Alessandro Michele-era Flora goods; sentiment is mixed between nostalgia and skepticism about material quality.

items.title: PurseBlog: Five Gucci Bags to Buy as a First Designer Bag in 2026
items.summary: US handbag community editorial recommends entry-point Gucci bags for first-time luxury buyers, reflecting continued grassroots enthusiasm and value-for-money debate among American collectors even as headline sales figures decline.

items.title: Louis Vuitton, Gucci, Burberry, Chanel, Prada and Dior Are Most Purchased Items on eBay in 2026 So Far
items.summary: WWD reports Gucci ranks among the top resold/purchased luxury brands on eBay for the year to date, alongside Louis Vuitton, Chanel, Burberry, Prada, and Dior, indicating sustained secondary-market demand for Gucci even amid retail sales softness.
